content: Hello everyone, I've been playing around with Kernel Modules written in Rust since a couple of weeks, but there's something I can't get working, and I don't even really understand if it's possible. I found this repository: https://github.com/elliott10/e1000-driver/tree/dev, which seems to work fine with crates, and even with external ones it seems. I'm just wondering something, would this work on a "normal" kernel? Or do I need a fork with specifics, I don't really understand. Same for the kernel crate, I've seen https://rust-for-linux.github.io/docs/kernel/ and https://rust.docs.kernel.org/kernel/ , but I don't understand, are the rust-for-linux docs just for a fork of the linux kernel? And the other "official" docs for what actually made it into the kernel?

Regarding third-party crates, please see https://rust-for-linux.com/third-party-crates.

Regarding the docs, https://rust-for-linux.github.io/docs/kernel/ is just an archive of old docs -- there is a red banner there that points you to the current one, which is what is in the mainline kernel. Please see https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#rust as well if you want to understand more of the context.

I hope that helps!
Hello, yes, I got my answers, thanks!
shard7 has marked this topic as resolved.
